items.q: How does a buffer maintain its pH when small amounts of acid are added?
items.correct: The conjugate base in the buffer reacts with added H+ to remove it, keeping pH nearly constant.
items.misconception: A buffer prevents any H+ ions from being added to the solution at all.
items.random_wrong: A buffer destroys added acid by changing it into water vapour.
items.why: Students treat 'buffer' as a magical pH-locker rather than a chemical equilibrium that absorbs H+ or OH- through reactions with its components.

items.q: What is the product of mild oxidation of a primary alcohol?
items.correct: An aldehyde (which can be further oxidised to a carboxylic acid).
items.misconception: A primary alcohol is oxidised directly to a ketone.
items.random_wrong: A primary alcohol is oxidised to an alkene by losing water.
items.why: Students confuse the products of primary vs secondary alcohol oxidation because they look structurally similar, but only secondary alcohols form ketones.

items.q: What is the difference between a strong acid and a concentrated acid?
items.correct: A strong acid fully dissociates in water; a concentrated acid has a high amount of acid per volume — these are independent properties.
items.misconception: Strong and concentrated mean the same thing for acids.
items.random_wrong: A strong acid is one that has been heated to higher temperature.
items.why: In everyday language 'strong' and 'concentrated' are synonyms (e.g. strong coffee), so students transfer that meaning to chemistry.

items.q: For the Haber process N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃ (exothermic), increasing temperature:
items.why: Correct answer: Decreases yield of NH₃. “Increases yield of NH₃” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Adding a catalyst to an equilibrium system:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Reaches equilibrium faster, same position. “Increases yield” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
